ddls:
CREATE TABLE "jdbc_connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each JDBC connection */,
  "connector" TEXT /* Type of connector used, typically 'jdbc' */,
  "url" TEXT /* The JDBC database URL */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* The name of the JDBC table to connect */,
  "driver" TEXT /* The class name of the JDBC driver */,
  "username" TEXT /* The JDBC user name */,
  "password" TEXT /* The JDBC password */,
  "forwarded" INTEGER /* Indicates if the connection should be forwarded */,
  "default" INTEGER /* Indicates if this connection is the default one */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the connection was last updated */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User who created the connection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_jdbc_connections_driver FOREIGN KEY ("driver") REFERENCES drivers ("driver_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "connection_params" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "param_type" TEXT /* Type of the parameter */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is required */,
  "can_be_forwarded" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter can be forwarded */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the parameter if any */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "drivers" (
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each driver */,
  "driver_name" TEXT /* Name of the driver */,
  "database_type" TEXT /* Type of database the driver is for */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Class name of the driver */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the driver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connection_profiles" (
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each connection profile */,
  "profile_name" TEXT /* Name of the connection profile */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JDBC connection */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the connection profile */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "connection_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JDBC connection */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the log entry was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the connection */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if there was an issue with the connection */,
  "stack_trace" TEXT /* Stack trace of the error if any */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_connection_logs_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES jdbc_connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "permission_type" TEXT /* Type of permission (e.g., create, edit, delete) */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JDBC connection (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "encryption_keys" (
  "key_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each encryption key */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JDBC connection */,
  "encryption_key" TEXT /* The encryption key used */,
  "algorithm" TEXT /* The encryption algorithm */,
  "key_type" TEXT /* Type of encryption key (e.g., symmetric, asymmetric) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("key_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_encryption_keys_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES jdbc_connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trails" (
  "trail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit trail */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JDBC connection */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change made (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* User who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the change was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trails_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES jdbc_connections ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the JDBC connection */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., error, warning) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Notification message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_connection_id FOREIGN KEY ("connection_id") REFERENCES jdbc_connections ("connection_id")
)
